name: bob-tasks
description: Auto-dispatch queued tasks to IBM Bob, with mode routing, a safety gate, defer-while-chatting, and native notifications.

bobTasks.connectorPath.description: Absolute path to the Bob Control connector install (the folder containing dist/worker.js). Set this once (User scope) so the worker can start regardless of which project Bob has open. Empty = auto-detect: any open workspace folder that contains dist/worker.js, else the legacy bobTasks.projectRoot if it still points at the install.

bobTasks.projectRoot.description: Working directory the worker runs in — the project Bob operates on (verify/judge run git here). The board DB stays with the connector regardless. Empty = the first open workspace folder. To locate dist/worker.js, use bobTasks.connectorPath instead.

bobTasks.nodePath.description: Node executable used to run the worker. Must be Node >= 22.5 (node:sqlite). Set an absolute path if 'node' on PATH is older.

bobTasks.dbPath.description: SQLite task DB (BOB_TASKS_DB). Empty = the project's data/tasks.db. Must match the MCP server's DB.

bobTasks.worktreeShared.description: Share ONE board across all linked git worktrees of a repo: this worktree's worker drains the MAIN worktree's data/tasks.db. Governs the WORKER only — to also make a Claude session running INSIDE a linked worktree file to the shared board, set the BOB_TASKS_WORKTREE_SHARED=1 environment variable (every consumer reads it). Ignored when bobTasks.dbPath is set; a no-op for non-worktree projects.

bobTasks.pipe.description: [Bob 1.x only — no effect on Bob 2.0, which has no IPC pipe] Bob IPC named pipe. Blank = auto-detect this instance's own pipe from ROO_CODE_IPC_SOCKET_PATH (needed when multiple Bob instances run at once). Set only to override.

bobTasks.maxRisk.description: Only auto-dispatch tasks whose mode risk is at or below this. 'advanced' (browser/commands) is elevated.

bobTasks.assignee.description: Assignee recorded when the worker claims a task.

bobTasks.tag.description: Only process tasks with this tag. Empty = all tasks.

bobTasks.autoStart.description: Start the worker automatically when Bob launches.

bobTasks.autoApproveGlobal.description: On the first 2.0 dispatch, write Bob's headless auto-approve into its GLOBAL settings (~/.bob/settings/settings.json) so queued tasks run unattended. This disables Bob's command security for every Bob window/project for your user and persists until changed (a one-time notice is shown the first time). Turn off to keep Bob's normal approval prompts — auto-dispatch will then stall on the first prompt.

bobTasks.notify.enabled.description: Show a notification when a task finishes.

bobTasks.dispatch.surface.description: Where dispatched tasks render. 'sidebar' = quiet same-tab; 'newTab' = isolated editor tab (steals focus).

bobTasks.dispatch.bringToFront.description: On each dispatch, bring the IBM BOB view to the front. Off = quiet, no window jump.

bobTasks.deferWhileChatting.description: Pause auto-dispatch while you are actively chatting with Bob, so the worker never aborts your live chat.

bobTasks.deferIdleMs.description: How long Bob's chat must be idle (ms) before the worker resumes.

bobTasks.commandClassifier.description: [Bob 1.x only — no effect on Bob 2.0, which removed the IPC button channel] Let Claude approve/deny commands that fall outside the safe allowlist (instead of Bob's manual prompt) for code, orchestrator, and advanced modes. Requires the Bob button patch (tools/patch-bob-buttons.mjs). Turn off to revert to manual approval.

bobTasks.answerFollowups.description: [Bob 1.x only — no effect on Bob 2.0, which has no IPC reply channel] Let Claude answer Bob's followup questions during a task (sending the reply over IPC), escalating to you when it's unsure. Off = questions wait for you. Uses the same backend as the command classifier; no button patch needed.

bobTasks.escalateAll.description: [Bob 1.x only — depends on answerFollowups, which is inert on Bob 2.0] Escalate ALL followup questions to you for review (including plan approvals), instead of auto-answering. Only applies when bobTasks.answerFollowups is on. Off = Claude answers confident questions.

bobTasks.reviewPlans.description: [Bob 1.x only — depends on answerFollowups, which is inert on Bob 2.0] Escalate plan/design-approval questions to you for review, while auto-answering mechanical clarifications (file paths, flag names, etc.). Only applies when bobTasks.answerFollowups is on. Off = Claude answers all confident questions. Takes precedence over escalateAll when both are on.

enumDescriptions: Run the installed `claude` CLI headless — reuses your Claude login (no API key), Sonnet-grade judgment, but each call is heavier (~full-agent cost).
One raw Anthropic API call — cheap and fast (Haiku), but needs ANTHROPIC_API_KEY in the environment.
bobTasks.classifierBackend.description: How the command classifier reaches Claude. Only used when bobTasks.commandClassifier is on.

bobTasks.classifierModel.description: Model the command classifier uses. Empty = per-backend default (cli→claude-sonnet-4-6, api→claude-haiku-4-5).

bobTasks.classifierCliPath.description: Path to the `claude` executable for the cli backend. Empty = resolve `claude` on PATH.

bobTasks.verifyAndContinue.description: After Bob completes a task, run an acceptance check and loop back to Bob to fix issues until it passes or max-continues is reached. Catches broken builds/tests without human intervention.

bobTasks.verifyCommand.description: Command to run for acceptance checks when verifyAndContinue is on. Empty = the check blind-passes (no verify command — not checked); enable bobTasks.verifyJudge for a real gate. Exit code 0 = pass, non-zero = fail.

bobTasks.verifyJudge.description: Use an LLM judge to verify task completion when no verifyCommand is set. The judge reviews Bob's work against the task criteria and actual code changes. Uses the same backend as the command classifier. When both verifyCommand and verifyJudge are on, the command runs first and the judge provides an additional gate.

bobTasks.maxContinues.description: Maximum number of fix loops when verifyAndContinue is on. After this many attempts, the task is marked as failed.

bobTasks.detectPlanStop.description: Check if Bob did real work (git working-tree changed) after completion. If the tree is clean (plan-only, no code written), auto-continue asking Bob to implement the plan.

bobTasks.maxRetryAttempts.description: Auto-retry transient failures (timeout/abort) up to this many total attempts. 0 = no retries.

bobTasks.allowCommands.description: Comma-separated command prefixes to extend the safe allowlist for advanced mode (e.g., 'git,npm test'). Empty = use built-in allowlist only.
